title: South Boston runner supports BMC social workers in Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Paige Beattie, 23, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to raise funds for Boston Medical Center's safety-net mission.
Beattie is inspired by social workers at BMC, emphasizing their role in providing housing and crisis care to patients.
She aims to raise $10,000 through various fundraising events to support BMC's mission and community care efforts.

title: Patience remains an elusive virtue for Legacy FC attack
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC lost 1-0 to San Diego Wave at Gillette Stadium, still seeking its first NWSL win.
Coach Filipa Patão emphasized patience and careful play in the final third, noting the team's rushed attacks.
Forward Amanda Gutierres struggled to connect despite 40 touches, highlighting offensive challenges for Legacy FC.

title: Melrose man charged with stealing hundreds of thousands from clients, prosecutors say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Henry Rappa Jr., a suspended Melrose attorney, charged with embezzling hundreds of thousands and practicing law unauthorized.
Rappa allegedly misused $660,000 entrusted by a client for a legal dispute, failing to return funds post-resolution.
He continued legal work despite suspensions, mishandling $21,300 from a real estate deal, authorities report.

title: Eugene Mirman posts message after fiery NH crash and dramatic rescue
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Comedian Eugene Mirman was rescued from a burning vehicle after crashing at Bedford Toll Plaza, New Hampshire.
Mirman suffered serious injuries and was rescued by a state trooper and New Hampshire Gov. Kelly Ayotte's security detail.
On social media, Mirman thanked rescuers and hospital staff, expressing gratitude and humor about the incident.

title: NH trooper assaulted in unprovoked attack
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Jonathan G. Newcomb, 53, charged with felony and misdemeanor counts after attacking NH State Trooper in Dover.
Attack occurred in a parking lot near Route 16; involved a stick and metal tool, state police report.
Both Newcomb and trooper treated for minor injuries; Newcomb facing numerous charges including assault and resisting arrest.

title: This runner ‘barely finished’ the Boston Marathon last year. Now he’s back to run again.
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Bryan Mallett, 24, is running the Boston Marathon again to support Mass General in honor of Sharla Sieve.
Sharla Sieve, who passed in 2022, was a patient at Mass General; a fundraiser raised over $123,000 for new programs.
Despite a calf injury in last year's marathon, Mallett aims to complete all major marathons, starting with Boston.

title: NFL executives reportedly ‘expect’ Patriots to acquire A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: NFL executives expect the Patriots to acquire Eagles WR A.J. Brown after June 1 for more favorable cap implications.
Brown, 28, is a three-time Pro Bowler and has six 1,000-yard seasons; Eagles seek a first-round pick for him.
Patriots made previous WR moves, signing Romeo Doubs, but still seek a strong WR1 following Stefon Diggs's release.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Testament of Ann Lee' starring Amanda Seyfried is now streaming on Hulu.
'28 Years Later: The Bone Temple', directed by Nia DaCosta, is available on Netflix.
Ken Burns' new documentary 'Henry David Thoreau' featuring Don Henley is streaming on PBS.

title: Caleb Durbin’s redemption, Willson Contreras’s swagger & other takeaways from Red Sox’ home-opening win
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox defeated the Padres 5-2 in home opener, ending a five-game losing streak.
Caleb Durbin broke out of a slump with an RBI single after replacing Alex Bregman.
Pitcher Johan Oviedo placed on 15-day injured list due to elbow strain.

title: Here are the dates for the Patriots’ offseason workout schedule
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots' offseason workout begins with phase one on April 20, focusing on conditioning and rehab work.
Organized Team Activities sessions are scheduled for May 27, May 29, June 1-2, June 4, June 8-9, and June 11.
Mandatory minicamp is set for June 15-17, concluding the offseason program before July's training camp in Foxborough.
